description: Document this plan as the exhaustive follow-up to both the original premium-ABAC plan and the earlier 2026-04-22 surgical remediation plan, with explicit historical traceability.
Make `ensureDraftBundleRevision(...)` transaction-safe so concurrent draft creation cannot fail on revision-number races.
Make draft revision creation and published-rule copy atomic so a newly created draft cannot be left partially initialized.
Tighten EE bundle write flows so `ensureDraft` and subsequent rule mutation or publish steps behave safely under stale-state races.
Prevent publishing empty or otherwise invalid draft revisions when that would silently remove narrowing.
Add explicit preflight failure for duplicate draft/published revision rows before lifecycle uniqueness indexes are created.
Provide or document a concrete repair path for revision/bundle drift or duplicate lifecycle rows that block migrations.
Prevent new assignments from being created against archived bundles and make assignment-status updates fail loudly on missing or invalid targets.
Decide and implement archive/unarchive assignment behavior so bundle archival cannot leave misleading active assignment state behind.
Decide and implement clone semantics for unpublished or draft-only bundles.
Introduce a shared quote-read authorizer for billing server actions so quote server-action parity matches the hardened API controller model.
Apply quote record-level auth to remaining quote read helpers, including versions, conversion preview, preview/render, PDF, and lookup-by-converted-record surfaces.
Apply quote record-level auth to remaining quote mutations, including update/delete, submit/request-changes, send/resend/remind, revision creation, and conversion flows.
Require quote item operations to validate both parent-quote authorization and item-to-quote ownership/integrity.
Fix `listQuotes` totals and page metadata so they reflect authorized results rather than page-local post-filter counts.
Replace remaining RBAC-only document URL helpers with kernel-backed document lookup and authorization.
Apply record-level auth to remaining document mutations, including update/delete, bulk folder moves, visibility changes, association changes, and folder operations.
Apply record-level auth to document content and block-content read/write/delete helpers.
Eliminate no-auth or RBAC-only document count leaks, including entity document counts, folder stats, and folder-tree count enrichment.
Replace or bypass `documentPermissionUtils` where it acts as a weaker, divergent authorization model.
Make folder trees, folder counts, and document summary metrics use authorized-document semantics only.
Introduce a shared asset-read authorizer and use it consistently across asset server actions.
Fix `listAssets` totals and page metadata so they match authorized rows.

Apply asset-level auth to all remaining asset reads, including relationships, maintenance schedules, maintenance reports, history, linked tickets, client maintenance summaries, entity-linked asset lists, and summary metrics.
Apply asset-level auth to all remaining asset mutations, including update/delete, relationship create/delete, association create/delete, and maintenance create/update/delete/history operations.
Decide and implement linked child-resource semantics for asset detail bundles, including whether linked tickets/documents require intersection with their own resource-family auth.
Finish `projectActions.ts` parity for any remaining phase/detail/status/count/tree surfaces that still rely only on RBAC.
prdRefs: Project / phase / task / status exhaustive remediation

description: Introduce reusable parent-project gating for task, checklist, dependency, resource-assignment, and ticket-link actions.
prdRefs: Project / phase / task / status exhaustive remediation

description: Apply parent-project gating to all remaining `projectTaskActions.ts` read and mutation paths.
prdRefs: Project / phase / task / status exhaustive remediation

description: Apply parent-project gating to all `projectTaskStatusActions.ts` and phase/custom-status flows, and add missing auth to currently zero-check surfaces.
prdRefs: Project / phase / task / status exhaustive remediation

description: Fix project count and summarization helpers so they do not leak task/status cardinality for narrowed-away projects.
prdRefs: Project / phase / task / status exhaustive remediation

description: Require cross-project operations such as move, duplicate, and link flows to authorize both source and target projects correctly.
prdRefs: Project / phase / task / status exhaustive remediation

description: Decide and implement structural-child semantics for project subresources so phases/tasks/checklists/status mappings inherit project auth while linked ticket data still respects ticket-resource auth where exposed.
prdRefs: Project / phase / task / status exhaustive remediation

description: Re-audit time/delegation flows beyond the prior `time_entry` resource-key fix and capture any remaining RBAC-only or aggregate leaks.
Re-audit non-API entry points that reach hardened resources, including file routes, previews, shared lookup helpers, and composition-layer actions.
Re-audit CE/EE helper seams so both sides use the same runtime semantics and do not regress into duplicated auth logic.
Produce an exhaustive surface inventory mapping file/function -> chosen auth semantics -> status -> validating tests.
Update the authorization baseline and cross-links so the final current-behavior ledger reflects the exhaustive sweep outcome.
Add bundle lifecycle concurrency and integrity regression coverage for draft creation, publish validation, migration preflights, and assignment governance.
Add quote server-action parity regression coverage for list totals, quote mutations, quote item integrity, and converted-record helper lookups.
Add document regression coverage for URL helpers, content/block-content actions, folder/count leaks, bulk mutations, and folder-tree semantics.
Add asset regression coverage for list totals, summary/maintenance/history/relationship reads, mutations, and linked child-resource semantics.
Add project regression coverage for phase/task/status parity, cross-project operations, and aggregate/count leak fixes.
